author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

title: John 1.1

source.title: NPNF1-14. Saint Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of St. John and the Epistle to the Hebrews

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Were John about to converse with us, and to say to us words of his own, we needs must describe his family, his country, and his education.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But since it is not he, but God by him, that speaks to mankind, it seems to me superfluous and distracting to enquire into these matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And yet even thus it is not superfluous, but even very necessary.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when you have learned who he was, and from whence, who his parents, and what his character, and then hear his voice and all his heavenly wisdom, then you shall know right well that these (doctrines) belong not to him, but to the Divine power stirring his soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From what country then was he?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From no country; but from a poor village, and from a land little esteemed, and producing no good thing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the Scribes speak evil of Galilee, saying, “Search and look, for out of Galilee ariseth no prophet.” ( John vii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And “the Israelite indeed” speaks ill of it, saying, “Can any good thing come out of Nazareth?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And being of this land, he was not even of any remarkable place in it, but of one not even distinguished by name.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of this he was, and his father a poor fisherman, so poor that he took his sons to the same employment.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now you all know that no workman will choose to bring up his son to succeed him in his trade, unless poverty press him very hard, especially where the trade is a mean one.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But nothing can be poorer, meaner, no, nor more ignorant, than fishermen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet even among them there are some greater, some less; and even there our Apostle occupied the lower rank, for he did not take his prey from the sea, but passed his time on a certain little lake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as he was engaged by it with his father and his brother James, and they mending their broken nets, a thing which of itself marked extreme poverty, so Christ called him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As for worldly instruction, we may learn from these facts that he had none at all of it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, Luke testifies this when he writes not only that he was ignorant, but that he was absolutely unlettered. ( Acts iv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As was likely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For one who was so poor, never coming into the public assemblies, nor falling in with men of respectability, but as it were nailed to his fishing, or even if he ever did meet any one, conversing with fishmongers and cooks, how, I say, was he likely to be in a state better than that of the irrational animals? how could he help imitating the very dumbness of his fishes?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This fisherman then, whose business was about lakes, and nets, and fish; this native of Bethsaida of Galilee; this son of a poor fisherman, yes, and poor to the last degree; this man ignorant, and to the last degree of ignorance too, who never learned letters either before or after he accompanied Christ; let us see what he utters, and on what matters he converses with us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it of things in the field?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Is it of things in rivers?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: On the trade in fish?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For these things, perhaps, one expects to hear from a fisherman.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But fear ye not; we shall hear nought of these; but we shall hear of things in heaven, and what no one ever learned before this man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For, as might be expected of one who speaks from the very treasures of the Spirit, he is come bringing to us sublime doctrines, and the best way of life and wisdom, [as though just arrived from the very heavens; yea, rather such as it was not likely that all even there should know, as I said before.]

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do these things belong to a fisherman?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Do they belong to a rhetorician at all?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To a sophist or philosopher?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: To every one trained in the wisdom of the Gentiles?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By no means.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: The human soul is simply unable thus to philosophize on that pure and blessed nature; on the powers that come next to it; on immortality and endless life; on the nature of mortal bodies which shall hereafter be immortal; on punishment and the judgment to come; on the enquiries that shall be as to deeds and words, as to thoughts and imaginations.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: It cannot tell what is man, what the world; what is man indeed, and what he who seems to be man, but is not; what is the nature of virtue, what of vice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Some of these things indeed the disciples of Plato and Pythagoras enquired into.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Of the other philosophers we need make no mention at all; they have all on this point been so excessively ridiculous; and those who have been among them in greater esteem than the rest, and who have been considered the leading men in this science, are so more than the others; and they have composed and written somewhat on the subject of polity and doctrines, and in all have been more shamefully ridiculous than children.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For they have spent their whole life in making women common to all, in overthrowing the very order of life, in doing away the honor of marriage, and in making other the like ridiculous laws.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As for doctrines on the soul, there is nothing excessively shameful that they have left unsaid; asserting that the souls of men become flies, and gnats, and bushes, and that God Himself is a soul; with some other the like indecencies.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And not this alone in them is worthy of blame, but so is also their ever-shifting current of words; for since they assert everything on uncertain and fallacious arguments, they are like men carried hither and thither in Euripus, and never remain in the same place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not so this fisherman; for all he saith is infallible; and standing as it were upon a rock, he never shifts his ground.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since he has been thought worthy to be in the most secret places, and has the Lord of all speaking within him, he is subject to nothing that is human.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But they, like persons who are not held worthy even in a dream to set foot in the king’s palace, but who pass their time in the forum with other men, guessing from their own imagination at what they cannot see, have erred a great error, and, like blind or drunken men in their wandering, have dashed against each other; and not only against each other, but against themselves, by continually changing their opinion, and that ever on the same matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this unlettered man, the ignorant, the native of Bethsaida, the son of Zebedee, (though the Greeks mock ten thousand times at the rusticity of the names, I shall not the less speak them with the greater boldness.)

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the more barbarous his nation seems to them, and the more he seems removed from Grecian discipline, so much the brighter does what we have with us appear.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when a barbarian and an untaught person utters things which no man on earth ever knew, and does not only utter, (though if this were the only thing it were a great marvel,) but besides this, affords another and a stronger proof that what he says is divinely inspired, namely, the convincing all his hearers through all time; who will not wonder at the power that dwells in him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since this is, as I said, the strongest proof that he lays down no laws of his own.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: This barbarian then, with his writing of the Gospel, has occupied all the habitable world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With his body he has taken possession of the center of Asia, where of old philosophized all of the Grecian party, shining forth in the midst of his foes, dispersing their darkness, and breaking down the stronghold of devils: but in soul he has retired to that place which is fit for one who has done such things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as for the writings of the Greeks, they are all put out and vanished, but this man’s shine brighter day by day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For from the time that he (was) and the other fishermen, since then the (doctrines) of Pythagoras and of Plato, which seemed before to prevail, have ceased to be spoken of, and most men do not know them even by name.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet Plato was, they say, the invited companion of kings, had many friends, and sailed to Sicily.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And Pythagoras occupied Magna Græcia, and practiced there ten thousand kinds of sorcery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to converse with oxen, (which they say he did,) was nothing else but a piece of sorcery.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: As is most clear from this.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He that so conversed with brutes did not in anything benefit the race of men, but even did them the greatest wrong.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet surely, the nature of men was better adapted for the reasoning of philosophy; still he did, as they say, converse with eagles and oxen, using sorceries.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he did not make their irrational nature rational, (this was impossible to man,) but by his magic tricks he deceived the foolish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And neglecting to teach men anything useful, he taught that they might as well eat the heads of those who begot them, as beans.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And he persuaded those who associated with him, that the soul of their teacher had actually been at one time a bush, at another a girl, at another a fish.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Are not these things with good cause extinct, and vanished utterly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: With good cause, and reasonably.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But not so the words of him who was ignorant and unlettered; for Syrians, and Egyptians, and Indians, and Persians, and Ethiopians, and ten thousand other nations, translating into their own tongues the doctrines introduced by him, barbarians though they be, have learned to philosophize.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I did not therefore idly say that all the world has become his theater.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he did not leave those of his own kind, and waste his labor on the irrational creatures, (an act of excessive vainglory and extreme folly,) but being clear of this as well as of other passions, he was earnest on one point only, that all the world might learn somewhat of the things which might profit it, and be able to translate it from earth to heaven.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this reason too, he did not hide his teaching in mist and darkness, as they did who threw obscurity of speech, like a kind of veil, around the mischiefs laid up within.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But this man’s doctrines are clearer than the sunbeams, wherefore they have been unfolded to all men throughout the world.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he did not teach as Pythagoras did, commanding those who came to him to be silent for five years, or to sit like senseless stones; neither did he invent fables defining the universe to consist of numbers; but casting away all this devilish trash and mischief, he diffused such simplicity through his words, that all he said was plain, not only to wise men, but also to women and youths.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he was persuaded that the words were true and profitable to all that should hearken to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And all time after him is his witness; since he has drawn to him all the world, and has freed our life when we have listened to these words from all monstrous display of wisdom; wherefore we who hear them would prefer rather to give up our lives, than the doctrines by him delivered to us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: From this then, and from every other circumstance, it is plain, that nothing of this man’s is human, but divine and heavenly are the lessons which come to us by this divine soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For we shall observe not sounding sentences, nor magnificent diction, nor excessive and useless order and arrangement of words and sentences, (these things are far from all true wisdom,) but strength invincible and divine, and irresistible force of right doctrines, and a rich supply of unnumbered good things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For their over-care about expression was so excessive, so worthy of mere sophists, or rather not even of sophists, but of silly striplings, that even their own chief philosopher introduces his own master as greatly ashamed of this art, and as saying to the judges, that what they hear from him shall be spoken plainly and without premeditation, not tricked out rhetorically nor ornamented with (fine) sentences and words; since, says he, it cannot surely be becoming, O men, that one at my age should come before you like a lad inventing speeches.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe the extreme absurdity of the thing; what he has described his master avoiding as disgraceful, unworthy of philosophy and work for lads, this above all he himself has cultivated.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: So entirely were they given up to mere love of distinction.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And as, if you uncover those sepulchers which are whitened without you will find them full of corruption, and stench, and rotten bones; so too the doctrines of the philosopher, if you strip them of their flowery diction, you will see to be full of much abomination, especially when he philosophizes on the soul, which he both honors and speaks ill of without measure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is the snare of the devil, never to keep due proportion, but by excess on either hand to lead aside those who are entangled by it into evil speaking.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: At one time he says, that the soul is of the substance of God; at another, after having exalted it thus immoderately and impiously, he exceeds again in a different way, and treats it with insult, making it pass into swine and asses, and other animals of yet less esteem than these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But enough of this; or rather even this is out of measure.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if it were possible to learn anything profitable from these things, we must have been longer occupied with them; but if it be only to observe their indecency and absurdity, more than requisite has been said by us already.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We will therefore leave their fables, and attach ourselves to our own doctrines, which have been brought to us from above by the tongue of this fisherman, and which have nothing human in them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then bring forward the words, having reminded you now, as I exhorted you at the first, earnestly to attend to what is said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then does this Evangelist say immediately on his outset?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: “In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God.” ( Ver.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou the great boldness and power of the words, how he speaks nothing doubting nor conjecturing, but declaring all things plainly?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this is the teacher’s part, not to waver in anything he says, since if he who is to be a guide to the rest require another person who shall be able to establish him with certainty, he would be rightly ranked not among teachers, but among disciples.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But if any one say, “What can be the reason that he has neglected the first cause, and spoken to us at once concerning the second?” we shall decline to speak of “first” and “second,” for the Divinity is above number, and the succession of times.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore we decline these expressions; but we confess that the Father is from none, and that the Son is begotten of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yes, it may be said, but why then does he leave the Father, and speak concerning the Son?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Why? because the former was manifest to all, if not as Father, at least as God; but the Only-Begotten was not known; and therefore with reason did he immediately from the very beginning hasten to implant the knowledge of Him in those who knew Him not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, he has not been silent as to the Father in his writings on these points.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And observe, I beg of you, his spiritual wisdom.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He knows that men most honor the eldest of beings which was before all, and account this to be God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore from this point first he makes his beginning, and as he advances, declares that God is, and does not like Plato assert, sometimes that He is intellect, sometimes that He is soul; for these things are far removed from that divine and unmixed Nature which has nothing common with us, but is separated from any fellowship with created things, I mean as to substance, though not as to relation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And for this reason he calls Him “The Word.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For since he is about to teach that this “Word” is the only-begotten Son of God, in order that no one may imagine that His generation is passible, by giving Him the appellation of “The Word,” he anticipates and removes beforehand the evil suspicion, showing that the Son is from the Father, and that without His suffering (change).

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou then that as I said, he has not been silent as to the Father in his words concerning the Son?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if these instances are not sufficient fully to explain the whole matter, marvel not, for our argument is God, whom it is impossible to describe, or to imagine worthily; hence this man nowhere assigns the name of His essence, (for it is not possible to say what God is, as to essence,) but everywhere he declares Him to us by His workings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this “Word” one may see shortly after called “Light,” and the “Light” in turn named “Life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Although not for this reason only did he so name Him; this was the first reason, and the second was because He was about to declare to us the things of the Father.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For “all things,” He saith, “that I have heard from my Father, I have made known unto you.” ( John xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: He calls Him both “Light” and “Life,” for He hath freely given to us the light which proceeds from knowledge, and the life which follows it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In short, one name is not sufficient, nor two, nor three, nor more, to teach us what belongs to God.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But we must be content to be able even by means of many to apprehend, though but obscurely, His attributes.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And he has not called Him simply “Word,” but with the addition of the article, distinguishing Him from the rest in this way also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou then that I said not without cause that this Evangelist speaks to us from heaven?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Only see from the very beginning whither he has drawn up the soul, having given it wings, and has carried up with him the mind of his hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For having set it higher than all the things of sense, than earth, than sea, than heaven, he leads it by the hand above the very angels, above cherubim and seraphim, above thrones and principalities and powers; in a word, persuades it to journey beyond all created things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then? when he has brought us to such a height as this, is he in sooth able to stop us there?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: By no means; but just as one by transporting into the midst of the sea a person who was standing on the beach, and looking on cities, and beaches, and havens, removes him indeed from the former objects, yet does not stay his sight anywhere, but brings him to a view without bound; so this Evangelist, having brought us above all creation, and escorted us towards the eternal periods which lie beyond it, leaves the sight suspended, not allowing it to arrive at any limit upwards, as indeed there is none.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For the intellect, having ascended to “the beginning,” enquires what “beginning”; and then finding the “was” always outstripping its imagination, has no point at which to stay its thought; but looking intently onwards, and being unable to cease at any point, it becomes wearied out, and turns back to things below.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this “was in the beginning,” is nothing else than expressive of ever being and being infinitely.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Seest thou true philosophy and divine doctrines?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Not like those of the Greeks, who assign times, and say that some indeed of the gods are younger, some elder.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: There is nothing of this with us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For if God Is, as certainly He Is, then nothing was before Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If He is Creator of all things, He must be first; if Master and Lord of all, then all, both creatures and ages, are after Him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I had desired to enter the lists yet on other difficulties, but perhaps our minds are wearied out; when therefore I have advised you on those points which are useful to us for the hearing, both of what has been said, and of what is yet to be said, I again will hold my peace.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: What then are these points?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: I know that many have become confused by reason of the length of what has been spoken.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Now this takes place when the soul is heavy laden with many burdens of this life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For as the eye when it is clear and transparent is keen-sighted also, and will not easily be tired in making out even the minutest bodies; but when from some bad humor from the head having poured into it, or some smoke-like fumes having ascended to it from beneath, a kind of thick cloud is formed before the ball, this does not allow it clearly to perceive even any larger object; so is naturally the case with the soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when it is purified, and has no passion to disturb it, it looks steadfastly to the fit objects of its regard; but when, darkened by many passions, it loses its proper excellence, then it is not easily able to be sufficient for any high thing, but soon is wearied, and falls back; and turning aside to sleep and sloth, lets pass things that concern it with a view to excellence and the life thence arising, instead of receiving them with much readiness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And that you may not suffer this, (I shall not cease continually thus to warn you,) strengthen your minds, that ye may not hear what the faithful among the Hebrews heard from Paul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to them he said that he had “many things to say, and hard to be uttered” ( Heb. v.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: 11 ); not as though they were by nature such, but because, says he, “ye are dull of hearing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For it is the nature of the weak and infirm man to be confused even by few words as by many, and what is clear and easy he thinks hard to be comprehended.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let not any here be such an one, but having chased from him all worldly care, so let him hear these doctrines.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For when the desire of money possesses the hearer, the desire of hearing cannot possess him as well; since the soul, being one, cannot suffice for many desires; but one of the two is injured by the other, and, from division, becomes weaker as its rival prevails, and expends all upon itself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is wont to happen in the case of children.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: When a man has only one, he loves that one exceedingly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But when he has become father of many, then also his dispositions of affection being divided become weaker.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If this happens where there is the absolute rule and power of nature, and the objects beloved are akin one with another, what can we say as to that desire and disposition which is according to deliberate choice; especially where these desires lie directly opposed to each other; for the love of wealth is a thing opposed to the love of this kind of hearing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: We enter heaven when we enter here; not in place, I mean, but in disposition; for it is possible for one who is on earth to stand in heaven, and to have vision of the things that are there, and to hear the words from thence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let none then introduce the things of earth into heaven; let no one standing here be careful about what is at his house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For he ought to bear with him, and to preserve both at home and in his business, what he gains from this place, not to allow it to be loaded with the burdens of house and market.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Our reason for entering in to the chair of instruction is, that thence we may cleanse ourselves from the filth of the outer world; but if we are likely even in this little space to be injured by things said or done without, it is better for us not to enter at all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let no one then in the assembly be thinking about domestic matters, but let him at home be stirring with what he heard in the assembly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let these things be more precious to us than any.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: These concern the soul, but those the body; or rather what is said here concerns both body and soul.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore let these things be our leading business, and all others but occasional employments; for these belong both to the future and the present life, but the rest neither to the one nor the other, unless they be managed according to the law laid down for these.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Since from these it is impossible to learn not only what we shall hereafter be, and how we shall then live, but how we shall rightly direct this present life also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this house is a spiritual surgery, that whatever wounds we may have received without, here we may heal, not that we may gather fresh ones to take with us hence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Yet if we do not give heed to the Spirit speaking to us, we shall not only fail to clear ourselves of our former hurts, but shall get others in addition.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us then with much earnestness attend to the book as it is being unfolded to us; since if we learn exactly its first principles and fundamental doctrines, we shall not afterwards require much close study, but after laboring a little at the beginning, shall be able, as Paul says, to instruct others also. ( Rom. xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For this Apostle is very sublime, abounding in many doctrines, and on these he dwells more than on other matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us not then be careless hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And this is the reason why we set them forth to you by little and little, so that all may be easily intelligible to you, and may not escape your memory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us fear then lest we come under the condemnation of that word which says, “If I had not come and spoken unto them, they had not had sin.” ( John xv.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For what shall we be profited more than those who have not heard, if even after hearing we go our way home bearing nothing with us, but only wondering at what has been said.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Allow us then to sow in good ground; allow us, that you may draw us the more to you.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any man hath thorns, let him cast the fire of the Spirit amongst them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any hath a hard and stubborn heart, let him by employing the same fire make it soft and yielding.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: If any by the wayside is trodden down by all kind of thoughts, let him enter into more sheltered places, and not lie exposed for those that will to invade for plunder: that so we may see your cornfields waving with corn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Besides, if we exercise such care as this over ourselves, and apply ourselves industriously to this spiritual hearing, if not at once yet by degrees, we shall surely be freed from all the cares of life.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Let us therefore take heed that it be not said of us, that our ears are those of a deaf adder. ( Ps. lviii.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For tell me, in what does a hearer of this kind differ from a beast? and how could he be otherwise than more irrational than any irrational animal, who does not attend when God is speaking?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: And if to be well-pleasing to God is really to be a man, what else but a beast can he be who will not even hear how he may succeed in this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Consider then what a misfortune it would be for us to fall down of our own accord from (the nature of) men to (that of) beasts, when Christ is willing of men to make us equal to angels.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: For to serve the belly, to be possessed by the desire of riches, to be given to anger, to bite, to kick, become not men, but beasts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Nay, even the beasts have each, as one may say, one single passion, and that by nature.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: But man, when he has cast away the dominion of reason, and torn himself from the commonwealth of God’s devising, gives himself up to all the passions, is no longer merely a beast, but a kind of many-formed motley monster; nor has he even the excuse from nature, for all his wickedness proceeds from deliberate choice and determination.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: May we never have cause to suspect this of the Church of Christ.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Indeed, we are concerning you persuaded of better things, and such as belong to salvation; but the more we are so persuaded, the more careful we will be not to desist from words of caution.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: In order that having mounted to the summit of excellencies, we may obtain the promised goods.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom on the Gospel of St. John.
verses.text: Which may it come to pass that we all attain to, through the grace and lovingkindness of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom and with whom, to the Father and the Holy Ghost, be glory world without end.
